column11: Camp Create is the City of Lakewood Parks and Recreation summer day camp held at Park Lodge Elementary School. Week 1 runs Monday through Thursday with drop-off at 8:00 AM and pickup at 4:30 PM. Campers ages 6 through 9 take part in activities, games, and field trip visits. Run by Lakewood Parks and Recreation staff. Registration is through the city's ActiveNet portal.

Adaptive Fitness Camp is a Metro Parks Tacoma summer camp at the STAR Center for youth ages 6 through 17 who benefit from adapted programming. The camp runs Monday through Thursday from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM the week of August 17. Activities focus on movement, fitness, and recreation skills appropriate to each camper. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

Around the World Cooking Camp is a Metro Parks Tacoma teen culinary camp at the STAR Center for ages 13 through 17. The camp runs Monday through Friday from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM the week of August 24. Campers explore recipes and cooking techniques from different cuisines around the world. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

# Batch import 2026-05-06: City of Lakew: Bibbidi Bobbidi Ballet & Bows Dance Camp
column11: Bibbidi Bobbidi Ballet and Bows is a Metro Parks Tacoma early-childhood dance camp at the STAR Center for ages 3 through 5. The camp runs Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to noon the week of July 27. Young dancers practice ballet basics, movement games, and themed dress-up activities. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

Boogie with Bluey is a Metro Parks Tacoma early-childhood dance camp at People's Community Center for ages 3 through 5. The camp runs Monday through Friday from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM the week of July 6. Sessions are themed around the Bluey TV show with movement and dance activities pitched at preschoolers. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

Bricks 4 Kidz Advanced Robotics Camp is a Metro Parks Tacoma STEM camp at the STAR Center for ages 9 through 14. The camp runs Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to noon the week of July 27. Campers work with LEGO-based robotics kits on building, programming, and project challenges. Run by the Bricks 4 Kidz program in partnership with Metro Parks. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

# Batch import 2026-05-06: City of Lakew: Bricks 4 Kidz LEGO Adventure Camp Afternoon (Eastside)
column11: Bricks 4 Kidz LEGO Adventure Camp is a Metro Parks Tacoma STEM camp at the Eastside Community Center for ages 5 through 12. The afternoon session runs Monday through Friday from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM the week of June 22. Campers build LEGO models tied to different adventure themes and complete guided engineering challenges. Run by the Bricks 4 Kidz program in partnership with Metro Parks. Registration is through the Metro Parks Tacoma ActiveNet portal.

PenMet Parks Tennis Camp is a four-day youth tennis camp at the Rosedale Hall and Tennis Courts run by Galbraith Tennis Center for PenMet Parks. Week 1 meets Monday through Thursday from 8:30 AM to 11:30 AM at the Rosedale courts. Campers play games and team-based drills designed for ages 5 through 10, with age-appropriate equipment and grouping by ability. Registration is through PenMet Parks' ActiveNet portal. Note: address is the Rosedale Park and Hall venue used by PenMet for tennis programming; verify on the registration page.
